name: PZB 90
summary: PZB is an intermittent train-protection system. Track magnets trigger acknowledgement requirements and speed-monitoring curves rather than continuously displaying an allowed speed.

controls.playerMeaning: Confirm that you have seen a restrictive warning or monitored speed reduction.
useWhen: Passing an active 1000 Hz magnet associated with a warning aspect or relevant speed reduction.
controls.doNotUseAs: It does not cancel the braking curve or grant permission to pass a stop signal.

controls.playerMeaning: Release from eligible 1000 Hz monitoring after the restrictive infrastructure has clearly been passed.
useWhen: The system permits release and the next signal or restriction is visibly clear.
No active 500 Hz monitoring or other reason prevents release.
controls.warning: Releasing too early can cause an immediate penalty if a subsequent active magnet is encountered.

controls.playerMeaning: Authorised override for passing a stop signal under a valid written or signaller's order.
useWhen: Only when the game scenario or signaller has explicitly authorised passing the signal at danger.
controls.warning: This is not a normal acknowledgement control and must not be used merely to avoid an intervention.

trainTypes.label: Lower
trainTypes.typicalUse: Trains with lower braking performance or operation in brake position G.

magnetFrequencies.purpose: Warning and braking-curve supervision after a restrictive distant indication or relevant speed reduction.
playerSequence: Acknowledge promptly when the indication activates.
Brake beneath the curve for the selected train type.
Expect monitoring to continue for distance and time even after passing the magnet.

magnetFrequencies.purpose: Stricter approach supervision close to a stop signal or severe restriction.
playerSequence: Approach at a low and reducing speed.
Do not assume releasing the earlier 1000 Hz influence removes 500 Hz protection.

magnetFrequencies.purpose: Stop enforcement at a main signal or protected point.
playerSequence: Stop before the signal unless explicitly authorised to pass.
Use PZB Befehl only with valid authority and follow the restricted passing procedure represented by the train and scenario.

restrictedMode.triggerSummary: After a 1000 Hz influence, remaining below roughly 10 km/h for more than about 15 seconds can place PZB into restrictive monitoring.
restrictedMode.playerMeaning: This often happens after stopping between a warning signal and a main signal. Acceleration remains tightly limited even if the next signal clears.
restrictedMode.warning: Exact indicators, release eligibility and implementation details vary between vehicles and TSW versions.

interventions.cause: No acknowledgement after an active 1000 Hz influence
Passing an active 2000 Hz magnet without valid override
Incorrect early release followed by an active magnet

tswNotes: Control names and cab indications differ between locomotives and multiple units.
Some tutorials simplify PZB operation; the website should describe the shared logic first and add train-specific notes separately.
The active magnet state matters. A visually restrictive signal does not by itself prove that the simulated magnet is active, and vice versa when game defects are present.
Resetting after an intervention is train-specific and should be documented on rolling-stock pages rather than treated as one universal sequence.

sources.title: Punktförmige Zugbeeinflussung
sources.notes: Initial monitoring values and operational summary. Replace or corroborate with public DB documentation where available.
